cfd_openfoam: No imposed internal Nu correlation; internal heat transfer follows from solved temperature field and wall gradients.
current_1d_model: Explicit internal_nusselt(Re,Pr,L/D) closure supplies Nu before h_i and R_i' are computed.
comparison_implication: CFD Nu/HTC is a postprocessed diagnostic; 1D Nu/HTC is an input closure.

cfd_openfoam: simulationType laminar; default noSlip wall velocity with selected slip NCC neighbor patches.
current_1d_model: 1D loop bulk-flow model with friction/loss closures; internal thermal closure uses scalar segment Re.
comparison_implication: CFD can resolve local velocity distortion; 1D uses segment-average flow quantities.

cfd_openfoam: laminar Fourier transport with tabulated salt transport properties in physicalProperties.
current_1d_model: Thermal resistance network with internal convection, wall, insulation, external convection, and optional radiation.
comparison_implication: CFD solves internal field; 1D composes scalar resistances.

cfd_openfoam: externalTemperature/rcExternalTemperature/zeroGradient in 0/T; prescribed h/Ta/Tsur/layers/emissivity metadata; no resolved outside-air domain.
current_1d_model: Natural-convection correlation plus optional linearized radiation and insulation resistance in 1D ambient-loss path.
comparison_implication: Neither current model solves a full external-air CFD domain; their external closures differ in form and parameters.

cfd_openfoam: Computed after the fact as wallHeatFlux_mean divided by wall_T_mean minus representative bulk temperature.
current_1d_model: Not postprocessed from field gradients during solve; h_i is predicted from Nu*k/D with optional multipliers.
comparison_implication: Do not present CFD effective HTC as the CFD closure law.

cfd_openfoam: Can be formed as Nu_eff=h_eff*D/k if diameter, bulk temperature, and conductivity conventions are fixed.
current_1d_model: Nu is directly computed before h_i, and nusselt_inner is exported in segment states.
comparison_implication: A clean comparison needs a shared convention for D, k(T), and bulk temperature.

cfd_openfoam: Not applicable as a CFD input.
comparison_implication: The 1D baseline embeds ideal-pipe/developing-flow assumptions that CFD may violate locally.

cfd_openfoam: Not applicable as a CFD input.
comparison_implication: The high-Re branch is available in the solver but Salt cases of interest should be interpreted through actual segment Re diagnostics.

cfd_openfoam: No equivalent solver input in the audited OpenFOAM dictionaries.
current_1d_model: baseline mode gives multiplier 1.0; per_parent_multiplier mode injects positive branch/segment multipliers.
comparison_implication: CFD-informed 1D forms must be labeled as fitted/diagnostic corrections, not CFD solve settings.

cfd_openfoam: CFD field/profile evidence can inform descriptors after postprocessing.
current_1d_model: profile_descriptor modes use shape and developing signals to form a bounded multiplier.
comparison_implication: Profile descriptors are model-form corrections that compress CFD patterns into 1D scalars.

cfd_openfoam: Cooler wallHeatFlux is a realized CFD boundary heat removal.
current_1d_model: predictive_airside_hx computes air-side/salt-side UA and epsilon-NTU cooler duty; imposed_qhx mode can prescribe duty diagnostically.
comparison_implication: Slide/report comparisons should separate predictive baseline from CFD-duty replays.

cfd_openfoam: CFD evolves coupled pressure/velocity/temperature fields in the OpenFOAM solve.
current_1d_model: 1D marches bulk segment temperatures using Q_source-Q_sink-Q_ambient over mdot*cp.
comparison_implication: Agreement can improve when CFD duties are imposed because the energy balance is then forced closer to CFD.

cfd_openfoam: Current effective Nu/UA/HTC closure observations remain coarse/no-GCI unless later mesh study admits them.
current_1d_model: 1D equations are deterministic once scenario inputs are chosen; uncertainty comes from closure validity and input support.
comparison_implication: Paper claims should mark CFD-inferred closures as diagnostic until mesh-UQ is available.

cfd_openfoam: CFD resolves salt-side heat transfer; effective Nu/HTC is reconstructed from outputs.
current_1d_model: 1D closes internal convection with explicit Nu correlations and optional CFD-informed multipliers.
comparison_implication: This avoids implying that both models use the same Nu law.
